document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical Evaluation Report Rev. 1 for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under initial CE marking.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to support EU MDR clinical evaluation, safety and performance assessment, equivalence assessment, benefit-risk evaluation, and acceptability of undesirable side-effects for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
keyPoints: The report was made in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.
The device is described as undergoing initial CE-marking and not yet placed on the market.
The device is classified as Class IIa under Rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, except infants and pregnant women.
The report compares the device with DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as CE-marked equivalent devices.
The report concludes that residual risks are acceptable and that the benefits outweigh the risks.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is digital imaging for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.evidenceText: Patient populations Adult and children except infants and pregnant women
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Population

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for mammography, infant and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, and angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device contacts intact skin surface and is not invasive.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is the device contacting skin.

evidence.claim: Patient contact with the front of the detector is less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Duration of use or contact with the body Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The device is reusable.
evidence.evidenceText: Single use / reusable Reusable

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa under EU MDR Annex VIII Rule 17.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this device is classified as Class IIa in accordance with the rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The report identifies DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as CE-marked equivalent devices.
evidence.evidenceText: No. CE Marked device 1 DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086) 2 CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197) 3 CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

evidence.claim: The report concludes DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 are equivalent to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: we concluded that the CE indicators ‘DRX-1’ and ‘CXDI-70C’ and ‘CXDI-31’ are equivalent to ‘Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector’

evidence.claim: The detector uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Technology

evidence.claim: The detector supports auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The device service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Service Life

evidence.claim: The report lists no known side effects for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Side-effects The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Side Effects

evidence.claim: Biocompatibility testing included cytotoxicity, irritation, and sensitization studies on the deco sheet.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of the test Cytotoxicity study... Name of the test Irritation study... Name of the test Sensitization study

evidence.claim: Performance testing was conducted for IEC 60601-1 basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Testing standard IEC 60601-1: 2005 /A1:2012 (3.1rd, CB Scheme) Testing laboratory Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: EMC testing was conducted under EN 60601-1-2:2015/A1:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Testing standard EN 60601-1-2:2015 /A1:2021 Testing laboratory Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The report states the benefits of the device outweigh the risks.
evidence.evidenceText: We have confirmed that the expected clinical benefit of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector... can be achieved through clinical data. The benefits of our device outweigh the risks.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation report updates are scheduled every two years.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document content primarily supports EU MDR/CE assessment and references Regulation (EU) 2017/745, CE marking, and 3EC (NB).

Classification section contains conflicting evidence: the criteria table states the device is not intended for recording X-ray diagnostic images, while the same section applies MDR Annex VIII Rule 17 for devices specifically intended for recording diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.
A/D conversion is inconsistent in the source: technical specification lists 16 bits, while the equivalence table for the device under clinical evaluation lists 14 bits.

Side-effect wording is potentially ambiguous: the device description says there are no known side effects, while the GSPR 8 section says literature research identified the most common side effects associated with the product without naming them.
Some source text appears to contain transcription or source-quality issues, including duplicated wording and a possible standard typo shown as EN 62222-1-1:2015 in one test section.
